title: Topological and arithmetic intersection numbers attached to real quadratic cycles
speaker: Henri Darmon
abstract: Abstract: I will discuss a recent conjecture formulated in an ongoing project with Jan Vonk relating the intersection numbers of one-dimensional topological cycles on certain Shimura curves to the arithmetic intersections of associated real multiplication points on the Drinfeld p-adic upper half-plane. Numerical experiments carried out with Vonk and James Rickards supporting the conjecture will be described.

proof_stages.label: Classical singular-moduli template and real-quadratic target
proof_stages.summary: The talk frames real quadratic cycles by recalling CM singular moduli, their Gross-Zagier factorizations, and their modular generating series, then states the goal of constructing p-adic real-quadratic analogues with similar class-field, valuation, and generating-series properties.

proof_stages.label: Rigid meromorphic cocycles as p-adic modular functions
proof_stages.summary: Replacing the complex upper half-plane by the p-adic upper half-plane, the speaker explains that the p-modular group acts non-discretely so invariant meromorphic functions are trivial, forcing the use of parabolic H^1 classes with values in the multiplicative group of rigid meromorphic functions.

proof_stages.label: RM evaluation and real-multiplication conjecture
proof_stages.summary: Rank-one stabilizers of real multiplication points allow a cocycle to be evaluated at such a point, divisors of cocycles define a field of definition via ring class fields, and the central real-multiplication conjecture predicts that these values lie in the appropriate compositum of ring class fields.

proof_stages.label: Construction and classification of cocycles with RM divisors
proof_stages.summary: Discrete negative-norm pieces of dense RM orbits give explicit product constructions in genus-zero cases, while a Knop/Choi-Zagier-style classification provides generators j_tau modulo scalars and identifies the H^2 obstruction to lifting them to genuine cocycles.

proof_stages.label: P-adic intersection invariants and arithmetic phenomena
proof_stages.summary: The p-adic intersection number J_p(tau1,tau2) is defined by evaluating the cocycle attached to tau1 at tau2, and computations show norm-one behavior, prime-dependent fields of definition, smooth denominators, and resonant valuations.

proof_stages.label: q-weighted Shimura-curve intersection conjecture
proof_stages.summary: After recalling the classical Gross-Zagier valuation formula via optimal embeddings into a definite quaternion algebra, the speaker formulates the real analogue in which q-adic valuations of J_p are given by q-weighted signed intersections of closed geodesics on Shimura curves attached to the indefinite quaternion algebra ramified at p and q.

proof_stages.label: P-adic modular generating series and class-field roadmap
proof_stages.summary: The final stage replaces norms by character-twisted products, conjectures that their p-adic logarithms are coefficients of a weight-3/2 p-adic mock-modular generating series, and relates this to a Hida-family derivative construction that may connect a p-adic Kudla program with Hilbert’s twelfth problem for real quadratic fields.

examples.name: Small singular moduli
examples.note: Class-number-one CM points and the class-number-three example over Q(sqrt(-23)) make concrete the classical fact that CM values of j are algebraic class-field generators.
examples.engagement_reason: He cites small/class-number-one and class-number-three singular moduli and states facts about their integrality and class-field generation without computing them.

examples.name: Golden-ratio cocycle evaluated at sqrt(223)
examples.note: The cocycle whose divisor is supported on the orbit of the golden ratio is computed at sqrt(223), producing degree-six polynomials that illustrate computability and the predicted real-quadratic class-field behavior.
examples.engagement_reason: He evaluates the golden-ratio rigid meromorphic cocycle at sqrt(223), reports computed degree-six polynomials for several primes, and checks their splitting fields.

passes.reason: The cocycle is attached to the golden ratio, singled out as the real quadratic irrationality of smallest discriminant, and is used as supporting evidence.

examples.name: Intersections of sqrt(2) with multiples of sqrt(2)
examples.note: The table of p-adic intersections for sqrt(2) and n sqrt(2) reveals smooth denominators and reciprocal valuation patterns, visibly motivating the Gross-Zagier-style factorization conjecture.
examples.engagement_reason: He computes p-adic intersection values for sqrt(2) against integer multiples of sqrt(2), analyzes their fields, denominators, and cross-prime valuation patterns.

examples.name: Discriminants 13 and 285 numerical test
examples.note: For D1=13 and D2=285, a computed two-adic intersection polynomial has prime factorization matching independently computed q-weighted Shimura-curve intersection numbers, providing numerical evidence for the valuation conjecture.
examples.engagement_reason: He runs a numerical test for discriminants 13 and 285, computing a 2-adic intersection polynomial and comparing its factorization with independently computed Q-weighted intersection multiplicities.
